station_address: 32, N S Patkar Marg, Babulnath, Khareghat Colony, Malabar Hill, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400007
text:
Beware of Fuel Meter Scams at This Petrol Pump
I strongly advise everyone to stay alert while refueling at this petrol pump. There have been multiple instances where customers were allegedly scammed through meter manipulation.
Always make sure to stand near the meter and verify that the reading is set to zero before the attendant starts filling petrol. Do not allow them to begin fueling while you are away from the meter. Once the process starts without showing zero, the reading may already include a previous amount, which means you could end up paying for fuel you did not receive.
The staff here seem very skilled at distracting customers during the process, which makes it easier for such scams to happen. Stay attentive, watch the meter carefully, and insist on seeing the zero reading clearly before the pump is started.

station_address: 32, N S Patkar Marg, Babulnath, Khareghat Colony, Malabar Hill, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400007
text: They are cheating people. Today morning I went to fill petrol and there were 2 attendants. I asked them to fill it to full tank and by the time I went to the drivers side to unlock the fuel tank and was coming back one of the attendants distracted me asking how will I pay and which card, etc. and as soon as I turned to check the meter it showed ₹800+ also I had seen the previous taxi guy had filled about 400₹. I argued with them on why they started the meter before showing me the meter was zero but they said sorry we forgot and stuff. Please do not go to this petrol pump or also don’t allow them to fill fuel until you check the meter

station_address: 32, N S Patkar Marg, Babulnath, Khareghat Colony, Malabar Hill, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400007
text: Be aware of scam here. Please stand near the meter and ensure that start filling up petrol only after you have checked the zero on the meter. They are expert in scamming. When asked for the receipt, I was given a receipt printed on HDFC paper when the petrol pump is of HP. I have seen many google reviews calling out the same issue. Assuming maybe the owner is also part of the nexus to scam people.

station_address: 32, N S Patkar Marg, Babulnath, Khareghat Colony, Malabar Hill, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400007
text:
Fraud alert.
We had asked for 1000 petrol.
But the attendant tried to trick by first refilling for 200 and then refilling for 800(actually 600).

When I asked for receipts of 200 & 800 from refilling machine.
They could not provide it.

Manager accepted the fraud and Apologies, but feel he is also involved in the scam.

Finally I paid only 800.

station_address: 32, N S Patkar Marg, Babulnath, Khareghat Colony, Malabar Hill, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400007
text:
Scam alert: Hi readers, please beware. There is a scam going here perpetrated by staff of this petrol pump. When my father asked them to fill the car tank, the staff only filled it for 500rs and pretended he was done. When my father repeated again that the tank be filled, the staff filled it completely. The bill indicator started counting from 500rs to 3,400 rs. However, when charging him, the staff along with his colleague tried to charge him 3,400 rs + 500 rs. Luckily, he was paying attention and strongly rebutted it. Later, on contemplating the chain of events, we realized that it was a planned scam and not a mistake.

Please beware!!

station_address: 32, N S Patkar Marg, Babulnath, Khareghat Colony, Malabar Hill, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400007
text:
These guys are cheats. I was asked to take my car a little ahead of the petrol dispenser, so that I cannot see the reading. I asked the operator to fill 3000 worth of petrol and while filling was on, the cashier took the money from me.
The operator only filled petrol worth 2000 and closed the lid of my car petrol tank.
I suspected foul play and rushed to the  dispenser and pulled up the operator. He said he made a mistake.

The guys at the petrol pump are all hand in glove and if they can make 1000 in 1 transaction, imagine the scale of money they make, in a day, in a month and in a year.

Attaching a picture of one operator who was involved.

Hope the authorities take appropriate action

station_address: 32, N S Patkar Marg, Babulnath, Khareghat Colony, Malabar Hill, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400007
text:
This petrol pump was our go to place to fill fuel in both of our cars untill the staff changed recently.
The new staff is very unprofessional. First of all they asked us to queue even though the other staff was free. Second they kept the fuel lid open meanwhile they were doing their admin work. We had to ask them to close the lid. Third they did not even show the Zero before starting to fill the fuel. Will definately avoid going here from next time.

station_address: 32, N S Patkar Marg, Babulnath, Khareghat Colony, Malabar Hill, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400007
text:
Filled petrol in my creta today around 520pm. Paid 4989/-. At home I checked & worked that he has filled 48 plus litres. Tank capacity is 50 litres, & I had already 8/9 litre in tank. Then I remembered, when he started filling, I was sitting in car, then I came down & saw his pipe was not in tank & meter was showing 4 plus litres & he told that there is some problem of gas in tank etc. And then he filled full tank, without interruption. I am sure he has not filled earlier 4 plus litres in car.
So please be careful

station_address: 32, N S Patkar Marg, Babulnath, Khareghat Colony, Malabar Hill, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400007
text:
Got SCAMMED by the classic FALSE RESET SCAM!!!
Ashish Churasiya- the pump attendant tried to distract me with everything possible and then claimed to misheard me that I asked for 500 worth of petrol when infact I asked him twice for a full tank. And my honda amaze never takes more than 3000 worth of petrol and he charged me 3500!! Even after confronting him for this blatant scam he didnt budge. Not to mention whenever I fill my car tyres it ends up with a puncture. Shadiest pump in Goregaon and pls be weary of your vehicle if you have to take it here at all. 🙏
PLEASE BE VIGILANT AROUND THIS PETROL PUMP.

station_address: 32, N S Patkar Marg, Babulnath, Khareghat Colony, Malabar Hill, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400007
text:
🚨Scammers 🚨

Please stay alert if you visit this petrol pump. Always check that the meter is at zero before fueling starts and keep an eye on the final amount and liters.

Today (24 May 2026) while returning from NESCO, we stopped here they asked us to move the car ahead and started fueling without properly showing the zero reading. Later, while one person distracted us by cleaning the windshield, another came with a payment machine showing ₹3500, even though the meter amount was ₹3062.

When we questioned him first he said fuel prices has been raised and this is the right amount but when we asked meter slip he suddenly said it was a mistake as he showed a diesel slip instead.

Please be very careful, avoid distractions, and always verify the meter and bill before paying.

station_address: 32, N S Patkar Marg, Babulnath, Khareghat Colony, Malabar Hill, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400007
text:
I would like to report an issue at the petrol pump. I asked them to fill petrol worth ₹3,000. First, they filled only ₹500 and said there was some problem with the nozzle. After that, they continued the meter from ₹500 and filled only up to ₹2,500, but they are saying that petrol worth ₹3,000 was filled.

I believe there was a mistake or unfair practice in the process. Please check this matter and take necessary action.

station_address: 32, N S Patkar Marg, Babulnath, Khareghat Colony, Malabar Hill, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400007
32, N S Patkar Marg, Babulnath, Khareghat Colony, Malabar Hill, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400007
text:
I had a very bad experience at this petrol pump today. The staff here are extremely dishonest.

I clearly asked them to fill petrol worth ₹2000. The attendant deliberately asked me to park my car ahead of the machine where the meter was not clearly visible. Although I stepped out and checked that the meter was reset to zero before they started, something still felt off.
The moment I sat back in my car, the attendant immediately said the tank was filled. I found it suspicious because it normally takes time to fill petrol worth ₹2000. Instead of leaving, I reversed my car and checked the meter myself — it showed only ₹1602.
When I confronted him, he went completely silent and then filled the remaining ₹400.
This is a clear attempt to cheat customers. Please be very careful if you visit this petrol pump — always check the meter properly and do not trust blindly.
I will also be filing a complaint in the consumer court regarding this incident.

station_address: 32, N S Patkar Marg, Babulnath, Khareghat Colony, Malabar Hill, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400007

text: Check the reading and what they type in the card machine. The person slyly switched my bill of 3165 to 3615. I quickly noticed and asked him to change back but have heard such complaints about this petrol pump before also.

station_address: 32, N S Patkar Marg, Babulnath, Khareghat Colony, Malabar Hill, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400007
text: I had a very disappointing experience at the Hindustan Petroleum near aarey Colony. Yesterday, we paid ₹1000 for petrol, but only ₹700 worth was initially filled without informing us. When we questioned this and asked for the bill, the staff casually admitted that the remaining ₹300 hadn’t been filled yet. The person who originally took our money walked away without saying a word, which felt highly suspicious. Only after we called another staff member did he acknowledge the remaining amount and complete the filling. Even then, we were given two separate bills. This felt like a clear attempt to mislead or scam customers, and had we not been alert, we could have easily lost money. Please be very cautious while refueling here and always double-check the meter and bill before leaving.
